political.s: Applebee's International, operating under parent Dine Brands Global (NYSE: DIN), made no direct PAC contributions to any federal candidate in the 2024 election cycle, per OpenSecrets data. No identified PAC affiliates were reported for the 2024 cycle, suggesting the company remains largely on the political sidelines. Applebee's, as a franchise-heavy model, has limited direct political spending compared to peers in the restaurant industry.

charity.s: Applebee's has maintained a 20-year partnership with Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation (ALSF), raising more than $16 million since 2005 to fund pediatric cancer research. In 2024, the brand raised more than $100,000 for the Bill Palmer Legacy Scholarship Fund benefiting restaurant team members and their children. Franchisees and corporate also collectively support veterans through free meals on Veterans Day, having served over 11.7 million free meals over 17 years.
charity.focus: Pediatric cancer research, military/veterans support, local community fundraising, employee scholarships

environment.s: Dine Brands' 2021 ESG report noted a 17.5% reduction in carbon emissions against a 2019 baseline across company-owned restaurants, and reduced expanded polystyrene packaging by 680 metric tons as part of a goal to be 100% EPS-free by end of 2024. However, as a nearly fully franchised brand, Applebee's has limited direct control over environmental practices at most of its roughly 1,600+ locations, and no standalone CDP disclosure has been identified. The brand's environmental footprint is substantial given its scale in meat-heavy casual dining with significant food waste and packaging use.

labor.s: Applebee's and its franchisees have faced multiple significant labor violations over the years, including FLSA minimum wage lawsuits, wage theft allegations, and EEOC settlements. In 2024-2025, an Alabama franchisee paid $270,000 to settle an EEOC sexual harassment lawsuit involving a manager and multiple female workers, including minors. Workers at various franchise locations have also alleged unpaid pre- and post-shift work and improper tip credit practices in multiple states.

dei.s: Dine Brands maintains six Team Member Resource Groups (TMRGs) as part of its diversity and inclusion efforts, sponsored by members of the executive team. The company has committed to the CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion pledge and pledged $100,000 to create diverse franchisee opportunities. Applebee's publishes a commitment to racial equity and has invested in diverse applicant interview panels and candidate slates.

animals.s: Applebee's is a casual dining restaurant chain serving meat-based dishes and is not a consumer product manufacturer subject to animal testing standards. Animal welfare in its supply chain is acknowledged in Dine Brands' ESG priorities, but no independent cruelty-free certification or animal testing policy for products exists.

guns.s: Applebee's does not sell or manufacture firearms. The chain has no disclosed firearms sales policy, and as a casual dining restaurant it is not in the business of selling or manufacturing guns. Some individual franchise locations may have local open-carry policies determined by state law.

privacy.s: Applebee's (via Dine Brands) collects a broad range of personal data including IP addresses, email addresses, location data, device information, and usage behavior through cookies and tracking technologies. The company shares personal data with franchisees, third-party service providers, advertisers, and affiliates across all Dine brands, and uses interest-based advertising through Google and Meta. While reasonable security standards and opt-out mechanisms are provided, the breadth of data sharing with franchisees and third-party ad networks raises notable privacy concerns.

execPay.s: Dine Brands CEO John Peyton's total compensation was approximately $5.42 million, with the CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio reported at 31.4:1 — significantly lower than the restaurant industry average due to Applebee's franchise-heavy model where most frontline workers are employed by franchisees rather than the parent company. This makes the disclosed ratio appear favorable but is not directly comparable to fully company-operated chains. SEC proxy filings confirm the ratio is disclosed annually.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
